artifacts.claimText: China Medical University's Academic Affairs guidance tells teachers and students to consider academic-ethics concerns before using generative AI and to avoid plagiarism and copyright violations in assignments, exams, and research papers.

citation.citationTitle: 生成式AI | 中國醫藥大學 教務處
citation.publisher: China Medical University Office of Academic Affairs
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: 生成式AI | 中國醫藥大學 教務處
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: 師生使用任何「生成式AI」工具前，應考量是否有違反學術倫理的疑慮。學術誠信是學習評量的基礎，所有作業、考試、研究論文必須遵守學倫理規範，勿抄襲，避免觸犯著作權法。
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Teachers and students should consider academic-ethics concerns before using generative AI; assignments, exams, and research papers should follow academic ethics, avoid plagiarism, and avoid copyright violations.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: China Medical University's Academic Affairs guidance cautions teachers and students to pay attention to privacy and information security and avoid entering personal or other people's private information into generative AI tools.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: 將「生成式AI」用於教學或學習時，師生應留意個人隱私與資訊安全，避免在「生成式AI」中輸入自己或他人之隱私資訊，以降低個人資訊外洩的風險。

artifacts.sourceTitle: 【重要宣導】中國醫藥大學 生成式人工智慧應用於學術研究之參考指引
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: 研究者在使用生成式AI時，應將其定位為輔助工具，切勿過度依賴或全然接受其產出的內容...研究者必須對AI生成的論述抱持審慎態度，務必親自查核相關資訊的正確性、可靠性與時效性。
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Researchers are told to treat generative AI as an auxiliary tool rather than over-rely on its output, and to personally verify AI-generated content for correctness, reliability, and timeliness.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: 一、強調原創性知識的建構; 二、嚴格驗證AI生成內容的準確性與時效性

artifacts.title: China Medical University Generative AI Guidance Staging Notes
artifacts.summary: Official China Medical University pages support narrow staged claims about generative AI teaching guidance, academic-integrity cautions, privacy and information-security cautions, and May 2025 reference guidance for academic research. Hospital AI application pages were excluded as research-showcase sources.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The staged claims are agent-reviewed candidates only and are not canonical public claims.
The evidence found is guidance/reference material; no central binding generative-AI policy document was verified in this crawl.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
